texto: Pressing my head to his heart, I listened hard, straining to hear any gurgle or murmur of life. Hearing nothing, I felt the shock settle into my mind, slowing it down and then turning it off."Don't leave me, Noah. Please, don't go," I whispered into the darkness as the light spray of rain touched my face.If only I could turn back time.I would tell him yes.
explicacao: Citação de Karen Ann Hopkins sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Pressing my head to his heart, I listened hard, straining to hear any gurgle or murmur of life. H…” articula amor, death, grief em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e dia-chuvoso.

frase_original: Pressing my head to his heart, I listened hard, straining to hear any gurgle or murmur of life. Hearing nothing, I felt the shock settle into my mind, slowing it down and then turning it off."Don't leave me, Noah. Please, don't go," I whispered into the darkness as the light spray of rain touched my face.If only I could turn back time.I would tell him yes.

texto: I hadn't thought about Mom as much as I probably should have lately. It was a relief not to have all those emotional waves rolling through me at the mere vision of her face in my mind. Letting go of all the negative thoughts was like blowing out a giant gulp of air that I'd been holding in for what seemed like eternity.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Karen Ann Hopkins: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “I hadn't thought about Mom as much as I probably should have lately. It was a relief not to have …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, death, depression pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e depression.
